codeReview.summary: Reviewed the prior failing review, the original failing commit c5482602de2aeb06bb4afaf87be5dd08e998cc1d, the fix commit b9a2e5870e8a82743d714ec8fa86dc9be413da3e, the fix handoff/transcript skeleton, and the current statcast client/test files. The fix adequately resolves the original refresh=False upgrade-path bug: both defensive fetchers now require matching sidecar metadata before reusing cached parquet files (src/clients/statcast_client.py:176-265 and 399-440), so missing or stale metadata forces a refetch with the all-player thresholds introduced by the prior fix. Regression coverage now exercises a fielding cache with no metadata and a catcher-framing cache with stale metadata, then confirms the regenerated caches are reused only after fresh metadata is written (tests/test_statcast_client.py:155-230 and 275-328).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The fix handoff reports a new repo-local stash, worker-4991f8d1 isolate unrelated model WIP, but .factory/library/workspace-state.md still does not record it. Future workers trying to recover or understand isolated local WIP would miss that stash.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The handoff records creation and retention of worker-4991f8d1 isolate unrelated model WIP at C:/Users/bills/.factory/missions/83c0c194-72d1-4821-8b08-68a3497c3590/handoffs/2026-03-19T16-44-36-660Z__fix-t6-cache-invalidation__4991f8d1-8422-451f-bc9c-101a6175cce2.json:26-28 and 51-69, while C:/Users/bills/Documents/Personal Projects/MLBPrediction2026/.factory/library/workspace-state.md:16-23 lists earlier stash entries but omits the worker-4991f8d1 stash.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared data-worker procedure remains out of sync with this repo's Windows workflow. It still prescribes generic pytest tests/ -v --tb=short and python -m py_compile src/**/*.py, so the worker again had to deviate to direct project-interpreter commands after .factory/init.sh failed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The fix handoff marks followedProcedure: false and records the Windows init/validation deviations in C:/Users/bills/.factory/missions/83c0c194-72d1-4821-8b08-68a3497c3590/handoffs/2026-03-19T16-44-36-660Z__fix-t6-cache-invalidation__4991f8d1-8422-451f-bc9c-101a6175cce2.json:16-18 and 77-93. The conflicting shared guidance is in C:/Users/bills/Documents/Personal Projects/MLBPrediction2026/.factory/skills/data-worker/SKILL.md:35-45, while the Windows-safe workaround is already documented in C:/Users/bills/Documents/Personal Projects/MLBPrediction2026/.factory/library/environment.md:57-61 and C:/Users/bills/Documents/Personal Projects/MLBPrediction2026/.factory/services.yaml:6-14.

summary: Pass. The fix adequately addresses the prior stale-cache failure by invalidating fielding and catcher-framing parquet caches whenever their sidecar metadata is missing or mismatched, so the default refresh=False path no longer keeps serving the original qualified-only data. I also noted two shared-state gaps: the new local stash is not recorded in workspace-state, and the data-worker skill still lags the documented Windows workflow.
